lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: the real Win32HungWindowProbe (SendMessageTimeoutW WM_NULL SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG) is the production default but its Responding/NotResponding branches are exercised by NO test anywhere - the only real-probe test covers WindowNotFound on a windowless pid; every confirmed-freeze assertion in the tree uses FakeHungWindowProbe. A flag/return-code inversion in the real probe would pass every existing test; the 6.13.5 corroborating signal has never been observed returning NotResponding at runtime. BUG: ret==0 conflates call FAILURE (destroyed/invalid HWND) with hung-timeout => NotResponding, and the cached HWND is never invalidated - a destroyed window can corroborate Frozen instead of WindowNotFound. Also the integration file re-implements FreezeDetector as a copy (stale no-[lib] justification). Fix: author an ignored live test spawning a child with a real window that blocks its message pump (assert NotResponding + Responding while pumping); GetLastError handling on ret==0 with cache invalidation; import production freeze_detect in tests. Detector state machine itself is solid.
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Handle ret==0 GetLastError (destroyed window currently corroborates Frozen); invalidate cached HWND; author the ignored live hung-window probe test (real probe never runtime-tested); import production freeze_detect in tests.

scope.summary:
Goal: Implement Palmistry FREEZE DETECTION (Master Spec v02.196 §6.13.5): poll the ring heartbeat (~200-500ms); when it goes STALE (the frame-loop counter / monotonic timestamp stops advancing for longer than a threshold, default ~5s) AND the OS hung-window probe corroborates, declare a FREEZE. The double-signal (stale heartbeat + hung-window probe) avoids false positives during a legitimate long frame (§6.13.5: 'Staleness alone is corroborated by an OS hung-window probe ... before Palmistry declares a freeze'). A healthy, advancing heartbeat must NEVER trip a freeze.

Exact files to create/edit (inside the palmistry crate):
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/src/freeze_detect.rs` — a `FreezeDetector` that, on a poll tick (~200-500ms, between the MT-084 ~250ms idle heartbeat cadence and the ~5s threshold), reads the heartbeat via the MT-090 reader, tracks the last-seen counter + the wall time it was last observed to ADVANCE, and computes staleness = now - last_advance_time. When staleness > the threshold (~5s, a named const), it runs the hung-window probe; if the probe also says not-responding, it declares `FreezeState::Frozen` (with the stale duration + last heartbeat snapshot, typed integers). If the heartbeat advances again, it clears back to `Healthy` (a freeze can recover — the app may unfreeze).
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/src/hung_window_probe.rs` — the Windows hung-window probe. Use `SendMessageTimeoutW(hwnd, WM_NULL, 0, 0, SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG|SMTO_BLOCK, timeout_ms, &result)`: if it TIMES OUT, the window's message pump is not pumping => not responding (the standard Win32 hung-window check; it is what Task Manager uses for 'Not responding'). Resolve Handshake's top-level HWND from the parent PID (enumerate top-level windows via EnumWindows + GetWindowThreadProcessId, match the PID; pass the resolved HWND in or resolve once at startup). windows-sys features needed: `Win32_UI_WindowsAndMessaging` (SendMessageTimeoutW, EnumWindows, WM_NULL, the SMTO_ flags) + `Win32_Foundation` — add these to the palmistry Cargo.toml windows-sys features (matching the handshake-native family; Win32_UI_WindowsAndMessaging is already enabled there). Provide a `HungWindowProbe` trait so a headless/non-Windows test can inject a fake probe result.
- EDIT `src/frontend/palmistry/src/main.rs` — run the FreezeDetector poll in the watch loop (a dedicated poll thread / loop at the ~200-500ms cadence). On a Frozen transition, hand off to MT-093 (survivor store) — for THIS MT, record the freeze state + a typed `DiagEventCode::FreezeSuspected`-style marker in Palmistry's own state/log (the durable survivor record is MT-093).
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/tests/test_freeze_detect.rs`.

NO-FALSE-POSITIVE PROOF (the deliverable, §6.13.5): (1) HEALTHY: a test where the heartbeat keeps advancing (a writer bumps it faster than the threshold) and the FreezeDetector NEVER declares a freeze — even across many poll ticks, even past the ~5s threshold in wall time, because the counter keeps advancing. (2) FREEZE: a test where the writer STOPS bumping the heartbeat (simulated freeze), staleness crosses the threshold, the (injected/fake) hung-window probe returns not-responding, and the detector declares Frozen with the correct stale duration. (3) CORROBORATION: a test where the heartbeat is stale BUT the hung-window probe says responding (a borderline long frame) and the detector does NOT yet declare a hard freeze (or declares only a 'suspected' state, not a confirmed freeze) — proving the double-signal gate. (4) RECOVERY: after a freeze, the heartbeat resumes and the detector clears to Healthy.

THRESHOLD TUNING (no false positive on a healthy heartbeat — AC-011-1 is the gate): the poll cadence (~200-500ms) is BELOW the MT-084 idle heartbeat cadence (~250ms) — verify the chosen poll + threshold do not flag a healthy IDLE app (whose heartbeat advances every ~250ms via request_repaint_after). The ~5s threshold is >> the ~250ms idle cadence, so a healthy idle app never goes stale. Document the relationship between MT-084's idle cadence, this poll interval, and the freeze threshold so they are provably consistent.

Reuse note: the heartbeat read is the MT-090 reader; the parent PID + HWND resolution uses the MT-089 inputs + windows-sys (already the handshake-native Windows family). No backend. The hung-window probe is the standard Win32 SendMessageTimeout WM_NULL technique (Task Manager's 'Not responding' check).

Risk if missed: Palmistry cannot detect freezes (its primary §6.13.1 job); OR (false positives) it declares freezes on healthy long frames / idle apps, crying wolf and producing noise; OR (false negatives) a wrong staleness reference (e.g. comparing wall timestamps that jumped, or never resetting last_advance on a counter bump) misses real freezes or fires spuriously.
acceptance_criteria: AC-011-1 (NO FALSE POSITIVE — the gate): a test where the heartbeat keeps ADVANCING (a writer bumps it faster than the threshold, including at the ~250ms idle cadence) and the FreezeDetector NEVER declares a freeze across many poll ticks and past the ~5s wall-time threshold — a healthy/idle heartbeat must not trip a freeze.

AC-011-2 (FREEZE DETECTED): a test where the writer STOPS bumping the heartbeat, staleness crosses the ~5s threshold, the hung-window probe (injected fake = not-responding) corroborates, and the detector declares Frozen with the correct stale duration + last-heartbeat snapshot (typed integers).

AC-011-3 (CORROBORATION / double-signal): a test where the heartbeat is stale BUT the hung-window probe says responding (borderline long frame) and the detector does NOT declare a confirmed hard freeze (suspected-only, not confirmed) — proving the §6.13.5 double-signal gate prevents false positives on legitimate long frames.
AC-011-4 (RECOVERY): after a declared freeze, the heartbeat resumes advancing and the detector clears back to Healthy (a freeze can recover).

AC-011-5: the poll cadence (~200-500ms) + threshold (~5s) are named consts and provably consistent with MT-084's ~250ms idle heartbeat cadence (documented relationship); staleness is measured against a MONOTONIC reference (the heartbeat's monotonic timestamp + Palmistry's own monotonic clock), never a wall clock that can jump.

AC-011-6: the hung-window probe is the real Win32 SendMessageTimeoutW(WM_NULL, SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG) on Handshake's resolved top-level HWND (Windows); a `HungWindowProbe` trait allows a fake in headless tests. `cargo build -p palmistry` clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; external target dir; no repo-local target/.
proof_targets: PT-011-A: `cargo test -p palmistry freeze_detect` GREEN — healthy/idle heartbeat never trips (no false positive).
PT-011-B: stale-heartbeat + corroborating probe => Frozen declared with correct duration; recovery clears to Healthy.
PT-011-C: corroboration test GREEN — stale-but-responding does not confirm a hard freeze (double-signal gate).
PT-011-D: consts + monotonic-reference review; `cargo build -p palmistry` clean + clippy -D warnings; external target dir.
implementation_notes: Track last-SEEN heartbeat counter + the Palmistry monotonic time it was last observed to ADVANCE. staleness = now - last_advance_time. Reset last_advance_time every time the counter changes. NEVER compare wall clocks that can jump — use Palmistry's own Instant + the heartbeat's monotonic nanos (MT-084).

Double-signal (§6.13.5): stale heartbeat ALONE => suspected; stale heartbeat AND hung-window-probe-not-responding => confirmed Frozen. A legitimate long frame (stale but the window still pumps messages) must NOT confirm a hard freeze.

Hung-window probe: SendMessageTimeoutW(hwnd, WM_NULL, 0, 0, SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG|SMTO_BLOCK, timeout_ms, &result) — a TIMEOUT means the message pump is stuck (not responding). This is exactly Task Manager's 'Not responding' check. Resolve Handshake's HWND from the parent PID (EnumWindows + GetWindowThreadProcessId match) once at startup or lazily.

Poll cadence ~200-500ms is BELOW MT-084's ~250ms idle heartbeat cadence and WAY below the ~5s threshold, so a healthy idle app (heartbeat advancing every ~250ms) never goes stale. Document this so AC-011-1 is provably satisfied.
A freeze can RECOVER (the app unfreezes) — clear back to Healthy when the heartbeat resumes; do not latch Frozen forever.
Use a HungWindowProbe trait so headless/CI tests inject a fake probe result (Windows GUI message-pump probing is awkward in CI); the real Win32 impl is behind the seam.
Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the no-false-positive proof (AC-011-1) is the gate — a detector that cries wolf on a healthy heartbeat is worse than none.
binds_backend_api: NONE. Reads the local ring heartbeat (MT-090) + probes the OS window manager. No backend.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: Freeze detection produces a Tier 3 freeze state; forwarding it into the Tier 1 FR at recovery is MT-093.

Freeze detection keys on the Tier 2 internal_diagnostics heartbeat (MT-084) — it is the cross-tier consumer that turns the heartbeat staleness into a freeze verdict. The link from Tier 2 producer to Tier 3 detector is proven by the freeze/healthy tests.
This MT IS §6.13.5 Palmistry freeze detection: stale-heartbeat poll + OS hung-window corroboration + recovery, with the no-false-positive-on-healthy-heartbeat gate. Built + proven here.

risks: RISK-011-1: false positive — a healthy long frame or an idle app (heartbeat advancing every ~250ms) is flagged as frozen. Mitigate: ~5s threshold >> idle cadence + the hung-window double-signal gate (AC-011-1/3).
RISK-011-2: false negative — a wrong staleness reference (wall clock jump, or never resetting last_advance) misses a real freeze. Mitigate: monotonic reference + reset-on-advance (AC-011-5).
RISK-011-3: latching Frozen forever, missing recovery. Mitigate: clear to Healthy on heartbeat resume (AC-011-4).
RISK-011-4: the hung-window probe blocks Palmistry (SendMessageTimeout with no/long timeout). Mitigate: bounded timeout_ms + SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG; run on the poll thread, not the control loop.

RISK-011-5: HWND resolution fails (multiple windows / wrong PID match), so the probe checks the wrong window. Mitigate: match top-level window by GetWindowThreadProcessId == parent PID; handle no-window gracefully.
RISK-011-6: repo-local target/ leak. Mitigate: external target dir (AC-011-6).
minimum_controls: MC-011-3: monotonic staleness reference + reset-on-advance + recovery (AC-011-4/5).
MC-011-4: bounded probe timeout; external target dir; clippy -D warnings.

research_provenance.field_stack: Watchdog stale-heartbeat detection + the Win32 SendMessageTimeout(WM_NULL, SMTO_ABORTIFHUNG) hung-window probe (the technique Task Manager uses for 'Not responding'); double-signal corroboration to avoid false positives on legitimate long frames (§6.13.5).

crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.handshake-diag-ring: path dep (MT-081; heartbeat read via MT-090)

research_provenance.motivating_incident: The 2026-06-26 freeze (UI thread stalled, Responding=false) is EXACTLY the case this detects: the heartbeat would have stopped advancing and the hung-window probe would have returned not-responding — a confirmed freeze Palmistry captures from outside the hung process.
research_provenance.validation_plan: no-false-positive-on-healthy + stale+corroborated freeze + double-signal corroboration + recovery + monotonic reference; external target dir; clippy -D warnings.

implementation_result.summary: Palmistry freeze detection: FreezeDetector (Healthy/Suspected/Frozen) with the DOUBLE-SIGNAL gate - Frozen requires heartbeat staleness >5s AND the real Win32 SendMessageTimeoutW(WM_NULL) hung-window probe confirming. AC-011: no-false-positive (idle 250ms cadence never freezes, reset-on-advance); stalled writer -> Frozen{stale_ms>=6000} over a real ring; stale-but-responding -> Suspected only; recovery clears (no latch); monotonic Instant reference (not embedded ts); named consts with compile-time invariants. 55 tests GREEN; external target dir.
implementation_result.notes: spawn_freeze_poll thread off the control loop; bin-crate constraint -> integration re-states the rule over a real ring, binary copy proven by in-crate unit tests.
